sense.definition: a small image that represents an individual or entity on social media platforms or online profiles

examples.x: I just changed my profile picture on Facebook to a new photo from my vacation.
Her profile picture on LinkedIn shows her in a professional suit.
I noticed that his profile picture is still the same old one from years ago.
He set a funny profile picture for his account to show his sense of humor.
A good profile picture can make your online profile stand out more.

sense.definition: the prominent and customizable image displayed at the top of a social media profile or webpage, often used to visually represent the individual or brand

examples.x: The cover photo on your profile looks great; it really captures your style.
She made her cover photo a picture from her wedding, and it looks beautiful.
For my business page, I created a cover photo with our latest product launch.
He made his cover photo a collage of his favorite memories.
I love how my friend uses a cover photo to promote her art business.

sense.definition: the personal details and other information that someone posts online on a social media platform

examples.x: She updated her profile with a new profile picture and bio.
His profile includes his interests, hobbies, and recent activities.
The social media platform allows users to customize their profiles.
She set her profile to private to control who can see her information.
He spent time curating his profile to reflect his professional achievements.

sense.definition: a short written description or summary that provides information about a person, often used in social media profiles or online platforms to provide an introduction or overview of oneself

examples.x: I updated my bio on Instagram to reflect my new job.
Her bio on LinkedIn mentions that she’s an experienced graphic designer.
You can learn a lot about someone just by reading their bio on a dating app.
My bio on Twitter is a short summary of my hobbies and what I post about.
I included my favorite books and movies in my bio on my blog.

sense.definition: a group of people who support or admire a thing or person, such as a public figure, group, or organization

examples.x: The artist gained a huge following after her debut album.
The political leader has a loyal following across the country.
The author’s books have a devoted following among young readers.
The band has built a strong following in Europe over the years.

sense.definition: something by which a person can show their interest in or approval of a post shared on social media, a blog, etc. by tapping a specific button
a spoken or written remark that expresses an opinion or reaction

examples.x: He replied to my comment with a detailed explanation.
I always read the comments to see what others think about the article.
I received comments from friends who enjoyed my travel photos.
She posted a question, and I replied with a helpful comment.
The comedian's post received numerous humorous comments.

sense.definition: a list of the online activities of a user on a social media platform in a chronological order
a temporary visual content shared on social media platforms for a limited duration, typically 24 hours, allowing users to share updates and moments in an ephemeral format
a piece of writing, image, etc. published online, usually on a social media website or application, or a blog

examples.x: I read an interesting post about cooking tips on a food blog.
She shared a funny meme on her social media post.
The viral post sparked a lively discussion among users.
They published a thought-provoking post on environmental awareness.
They shared a post to raise awareness about an upcoming charity event.

sense.definition: information that one shares on social media to show their current mood, opinion, or situation

examples.x: His status reflected his excitement about the upcoming concert.
She posted a happy status about her recent vacation.
Many people liked and commented on her status update.
He updated his status to announce his new job.
Her status received mixed reactions from her followers.

sense.definition: a personalized and constantly updated stream of content from accounts that a user follows, allowing them to view and engage with the latest posts, photos, and videos in a central place
a message sent on a social media platform that is available only to the recipient
an alert or message sent to inform a user about an event or activity on a social media platform or digital service

examples.x: She received a notification about a new message on her phone.
The app sent a notification when the update was available.
He dismissed the notification without reading it.
You’ll get a notification when your order is shipped.
She was overwhelmed by the constant notifications from various apps.

sense.definition: a person that one adds to the list of contacts of their account on the social media
a request sent through social media platforms or online networks, inviting someone to connect and add them as a friend or contact

examples.x: She received a friend request from a former classmate on Facebook.
After meeting at the conference, he sent her a friend request on LinkedIn.
He hesitated before accepting the friend request from his coworker on Instagram.
She ignored the friend request from the unfamiliar user on Twitter.
Sending a personalized message along with the friend request can increase the likelihood of it being accepted.

sense.definition: the level of interaction, involvement, or participation that users have with content on a social media platform, such as likes, comments, shares, and other forms of active engagement that indicate user interest and involvement

a chronological record or history of a user's actions, interactions, and activities on a social media platform, providing a detailed log of their past engagements, posts, comments, likes, and other interactions for reference or review

examples.x: The activity log helped her find the post she accidentally liked.
The activity log showed all the comments he made on his friends' posts last week.
She used the activity log to delete an old status update she no longer wanted on her profile.
His activity log helped him remember which groups he recently joined.

sense.definition: a feature on social media platforms that allows users to showcase or emphasize specific content by making it more prominent or visible
a private message sent on a social media platform, online forum, etc.
a message or post on Twitter

examples.x: Her latest tweet went viral, gaining thousands of likes and retweets within hours.
He often shares interesting articles and thoughts in his tweet to engage with his followers.
The tweet from the celebrity sparked a lot of discussion among fans and media outlets.
I received a notification that someone liked my tweet about the recent sports game.
The company's official tweet announced the launch of their new product line.

sense.definition: a form of video content, often published on websites or social media platforms, where individuals or content creators share their personal experiences, expertise, or opinions through recorded videos in a blog-like format
the act of updating a blog or a social media account by short videos on a regular basis

examples.x: The rise of vlogging has created new opportunities for content creators to monetize their work.

sense.definition: a dedicated online community or space where users with shared interests can join, interact, and collaborate in discussions and content sharing
a large group of people organized using social media who gather together publicly at an exact time, do something for a short time and then disperse

examples.x: I saw a flash mob in the park yesterday, and it was such a fun surprise.
The flash mob at the concert was so well-coordinated that it looked like a professional performance.
We joined a flash mob to raise awareness for the environmental cause.
It was amazing to watch a flash mob perform in the middle of a busy city street.

sense.definition: an act of organizing a large group of people using social media to gather them together publicly at an exact time, doing something for a short time and then dispersing

examples.x: Flash mobbing has become a popular way for people to express creativity in public spaces.
The flash mobbing at the shopping center drew a large crowd of curious onlookers.
Flash mobbing can be a powerful tool for raising awareness about important causes.
The flash mobbing was so well coordinated, it felt like a professional performance.

sense.definition: personalized suggestions provided to users on social media platforms and other digital services, based on their preferences and interactions

examples.x: The app's recommendation for new music matched her taste perfectly.
He checked the streaming service's recommendations for movies to watch next.
Based on her shopping history, the website's recommendations were spot on.
The social media platform's recommendation helped him discover new friends with similar interests.
She found a new favorite book through the app's personalized recommendations.

sense.definition: a set of rules used by digital systems to decide what content to show users based on their behavior and preferences

examples.x: The search engine uses complex algorithms to rank web pages based on relevance and authority.
Social media platforms use algorithms to personalize users' news feeds, showing them content based on their interests and behavior.
Online retailers use recommendation algorithms to suggest products to customers based on their browsing and purchasing history.
Streaming services use algorithms to predict user preferences and recommend movies and TV shows they may enjoy.
News websites use algorithms to curate and prioritize articles for readers based on factors such as timeliness and popularity.

sense.definition: a digital platform or online service that enables individuals to connect, communicate, and interact with others, allowing users to create profiles, share content, and engage in social interactions within a virtual community

examples.x: She uses her social network to share her photography and get feedback from others.
I joined a new social network to connect with professionals in my field.
My social network is full of friends and family, and I love staying in touch with them.
He spends a lot of time on his social network, posting updates and liking others' photos.
I met a lot of interesting people through my social network who share similar hobbies.

sense.definition: using websites and apps to interact and build social relationships

examples.x: Social networking has become an essential tool for businesses to engage with their customers.
She spends a lot of time on social networking sites to stay connected with friends and family.
The rise of social networking has changed the way people communicate and share information.
Many professionals use social networking platforms to expand their career opportunities.
The company's marketing strategy includes a strong focus on social networking to reach a wider audience.

sense.definition: any form of information, media, or material that is created, published, or shared on digital platforms, including text, images, videos, articles, podcasts, and more

examples.x: The website updates its content daily to keep users engaged.
She creates digital content for social media marketing campaigns.
High-quality content can improve a brand’s online presence.
Many influencers rely on engaging content to grow their audience.
The platform allows users to upload various types of content, including videos and blogs.

sense.definition: videos that are uploaded and played directly within the social media platform, rather than being shared from external video hosting sites

examples.x: I prefer watching native videos on Facebook because they load faster than links from other websites.
The company posted a native video on Twitter to promote their new product.
Native videos on social media platforms tend to get more engagement than links to YouTube videos.
He shared a native video on his profile to give a sneak peek of his upcoming project.
I noticed that native videos on LinkedIn appear higher in my feed than other types of posts.

sense.definition: a feature or format found in some social media platforms, such as Instagram, that allows users to create and share short, vertical videos in a scrollable, full-screen format
a feature on social media platforms or websites that allows users to store and access their past posts, stories, or content in a separate and organized collection
a video, image, etc. that has been altered to act as a joke or to tease someone, used and quickly spread by internet users

examples.x: She created a hilarious meme featuring her pet that quickly became a internet sensation.
The meme went viral on social media, garnering thousands of shares and likes.
Memes are often used to convey humor, satire, or cultural references in a concise and shareable format.
The meme featured a popular catchphrase from a television show, accompanied by a relevant image.
He shared a meme with his friends that perfectly captured their inside joke.

sense.definition: the anxiety or unease that arises from feeling left out of a social experience or opportunity
a photo of a person that is taken by the same person, usually shared on social media

examples.x: She snapped a quick selfie with her friends at the beach to capture the moment.
Selfies have become a ubiquitous form of self-expression on social media platforms.
The selfie received hundreds of likes and comments from followers on her Instagram account.
Taking a selfie with the scenic backdrop of the mountains was a highlight of their hiking trip.
She practiced her best smile before taking a selfie to share with her family.

sense.definition: someone's username on an online forum or social media platform

examples.x: His Twitter handle is @TechEnthusiast123, reflecting his interest in technology.
Choosing a unique handle helps users distinguish themselves in online communities.
Protecting your handle with a strong password prevents unauthorized access to your accounts.
The celebrity's handle has millions of followers, making them a prominent influencer.
A memorable handle can enhance personal branding and online visibility.

sense.definition: (computing) someone who posts hostile, irrelevant, or offensive comments on a website or social media to annoy others

examples.x: The troll's comments disrupted the constructive discussion in the thread.
Engaging with trolls only encourages them to continue their disruptive behavior.
The forum moderators banned the troll for repeatedly posting inflammatory comments.

sense.definition: the expression of negative sentiment or a lack of approval towards a post, comment, or content on social media platforms, serving as the opposite of the "like" button
an image that is the representation of a player in a game or an account on social media

examples.x: She created a colorful avatar to represent her in the online game.
His avatar in the virtual world looked just like him, with similar clothes and hairstyle.
Players can customize their avatar with different outfits and accessories.
The avatar danced around the screen during the interactive party.
She chose a cute animal as her avatar for the social media platform.

sense.definition: a tool or feature used on social media platforms or photo editing apps to modify or enhance photos and videos by applying various effects or adjustments for a desired visual outcome
a small digital icon or image used on social media or in text messages to show the tone of a message or its sender's facial expression, originated in Japan

examples.x: He used a thumbs-up emoji to indicate his approval of the plan.
She sent a series of laughing emoji in response to the funny meme shared by her friend.
